sources_exist.note: All seven canonical citation occurrences resolve to the intended subject pages. Identity matches the manifest. However, deterministic inputs fail separately: role_mix matches, while dataset_inputs.labor_share, dataset_inputs.n_total, and dataset_inputs.n_band do not copy the supplied non-null chains exactly.

figures_match_sources.note: The pages support some surrounding facts, including Scope's segment-level multiple ranges, myEZCare's 24% AI-investment benchmark, Vertess's qualitative succession commentary, CMS's PDGM mechanics, and CT's approximately 10.2x Enhabit reference. They do not support the score-bearing 11% adoption rate, EVV/EMR timeline, 38% owner-age estimate, exact ten-consolidator count, 6.5x buy median, 10.5x exit anchor as stated, or the payer mix and payment/cost figures.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The role-level automation fractions appropriately keep hands-on aide and clinician work near zero and concentrate automation in documentation and administration; pi_dist=0.30 and pi_moat=0.40 are directionally plausible. But t50_years=8 is built from an unsupported 11% baseline and unsupported historical analog, while owners_60plus_pct=0.38, the succession bonus, active_consolidators=10, and the selected multiple inputs are not auditable from the cited pages.

rubric_consistent.note: The record recognizes NAICS heterogeneity, identifies Medicare-certified skilled home health as the dominant band-size subsegment, avoids using deal activity in V or A, and handles the hands-on-care billing-unit trap sensibly. It nevertheless violates the mandatory exact-copy rule for three deterministic dataset inputs and substitutes blended or midpoint estimates for sourced medians in buy_mult and exit_mult. The arithmetic-delta flag is benign because the listed recomputation differences remain within orchestrator tolerance, but it does not cure the evidence failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: PRESSURED is a defensible terminal classification and the explanation candidly distinguishes temporary prospective-payment savings from later CMS rate adjustments. However, no_proxy_confirmed incorrectly presents the 11% production-adoption baseline and EVV/EMR trajectory as established. pricing_model_deflation also overstates four consecutive cuts and treats a heterogeneous payer mix as quantified even though the cited CMS page supports only Medicare HH PPS mechanics, not the claimed revenue shares.

source_audits.note: The tracker reports 32 in its title and 25+ in its quick answer across home health, home care, and hospice, using an institutional-sponsor definition that includes PE, public parents, holding companies, and ESOPs. It does not establish the record's exact ten home-health-specific active-acquirer count, and at least HarmonyCares is absent while Agape is identified as a hospice platform.

Scope reports 3x-6x for small skilled home health, 6x-9x for regional skilled operators, 3x-5x for small personal care, and 5x-8x for regional personal care. It does not report 7x-10x for the stated mid-size HHA bracket, a 6.5x median actually paid, or the record's unsourced blend across heterogeneous segments.

The page says fewer than one in four organizations have made an AI-specific investment and later gives 24%, with early adopters reporting efficiency gains above 25%. It contains no 11% full-production adoption rate and no 19% share self-identifying as early adopters.

The tracker supports Enhabit at approximately 10.2x EBITDA and gives 10x-14x for platform-tier HHA businesses. It does not give the stated 9x-12x HHA exit range or report 10.5x as an evidence-based median; calling 10.5x the conservative midpoint is also inconsistent with the midpoint of either stated range.

The cited CMS Home Health PPS page explains Medicare home-health prospective payment. It does not document Electronic Visit Verification requirements or near-universal state adoption by 2020/2023, and it does not contain the claimed voluntary home-health EMR trajectory from below 5% in 2010 to about 50% in 2020.

Vertess qualitatively says an aging base of independent owners is contributing to consolidation and succession planning. It gives no 60%-over-55 statistic, no basis for converting that claim to 38% age 60+, and no documentation that a shortage of successors exists; aide staffing difficulty is not successor evidence.

CMS supports PDGM's 30-day payment unit, the 2025 -1.975% permanent adjustment, and a net 0.5% aggregate payment increase. It does not report the record's Medicare/Medicaid/MA/private-pay revenue mix, an approximately $1,928 standardized rate, the $2,010.69 versus $1,527.23 comparison, a 32% excess, or the quoted more-than-a-decade history.

reasons: At dataset_inputs.labor_share, dataset_inputs.n_total, and dataset_inputs.n_band, the run does not copy the non-null supplied chains from pipeline/datasets/derived/621610.json exactly: labor_share replaces sources/year/quality with a singular source field, n_total omits year/quality, and n_band omits margin_used/margin_source/quality. Re-run with every supplied deterministic object copied verbatim.

At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.myezcare.com/2026-home-care-industry-report-workforce-ai-adoption-growth-trends/), the page supports a 24% AI-investment benchmark but not 11% in full production or 19% early adopters. Re-run with a population-matched material-adoption statistic and recompute t50_years from that sourced baseline.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.cms.gov/medicare/payment/prospective-payment-systems/home-health), the page does not support either the EVV adoption timeline or the voluntary home-health EMR trajectory. Re-run with direct dated EVV and home-health technology-adoption sources and a reproducible path to 50%; do not infer 8 years from uncited five-percentage-point annual growth.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://vertess.com/blog/how-much-your-home-health-care-business-worth-2026), the qualitative aging-owner statement does not support 38% age 60+ or a documented successor shortage. Re-run with owner-specific demographic data and direct evidence of an insufficient successor pool before applying the OW bonus.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/home-health-pe-rollup-tracker-2026/), the broad tracker does not substantiate exactly ten PE-backed home-health consolidators meeting the recent-add-on test, and the cited list mixes public parents and hospice operators. Re-run with sponsor status and a qualifying last-24-month add-on citation for each counted platform.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://www.scoperesearch.co/post/home-health-valuation-multiples-and-m-a-trends-2025), the page supplies segment and size ranges but no 6.5x median or documented blended statistic for the scored band. Re-run with the median actually paid for normalized-EBITDA targets in the dominant subsegment, keeping heterogeneous personal-care observations separate.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/home-health-pe-rollup-tracker-2026/), the page supports an approximately 10.2x Enhabit transaction and a 10x-14x platform range, not the quoted 9x-12x range or a 10.5x median. Re-run with a sourced exit statistic for comparable platform assets rather than an arbitrary midpoint.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.cms.gov/newsroom/fact-sheets/calendar-year-cy-2025-home-health-prospective-payment-system-final-rule-fact-sheet-cms-1803-f), CMS supports Medicare PDGM mechanics but not the payer revenue shares or the quoted payment-versus-cost figures. Re-run with separate representative payer-mix evidence and primary rate/cost sources before fixing pi_dist and the pricing-model cross-check.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S, V
